Title: Mediterranean Tomato Landraces Exhibit Genotype-Specific Transcriptomic Responses to Water Stress.
Summary: The study compares drought-tolerant Mediterranean landraces ('de Ramellet', 'Lucariello') with commercial varieties ('Moneymaker', 'Ailsa Craig') under water stress conditions using RNA-Seq. Findings show that transcriptomic responses are genotype-dependent and tissue-dependent, with landraces showing targeted drought-related pathways while commercial genotypes regulate metabolic pathways indicative of general impairment.
Relevance_Explanation: The paper matches the query perfectly as it focuses on Solanum lycopersicum (tomato), specifically analyzing drought stress in roots and leaves using RNA-Seq, comparing landraces to commercial varieties.
